summary: Language doctrine in root CLAUDE.md amended. Previous rule said 'code blocks quoting agent speech in templates are EN baseline, translated live at runtime'. The EN-baseline constraint on illustrative quoted examples was not enforced in practice and produced no operator-visible effect (runtime adapts to operator language regardless). Rule now explicitly authorizes FR or EN for quoted agent-speech examples inside skills, commands, and doctrine. Template prose (doctrine, system docs, README, vision, product) remains EN baseline.

changes.rationale: 63 template files contained FR-authored quoted agent-speech examples. Audit confirmed these have zero operator-facing impact (runtime translates anyway). Rule was a constraint without value. Amendment aligns doctrine with reality and removes false debt.

operator_impact: None. Quoted examples in skills, commands, and doctrine continue to render in operator language at runtime.
